title: Lorentzian polynomials and the incidence geometry of tropical linear spaces
why_relevant: This is the most directly relevant paper: it introduces relative Dressians Dr(M), identifies tropical subspaces of Trop(M) with valuated matroid quotients, studies their poset/incidence geometry, and introduces adjoints for tropical linear spaces.

title: Moduli spaces of codimension-one subspaces in a linear variety and their tropicalization
why_relevant: Studies the moduli space of codimension-one linear subspaces inside a fixed linear variety and its tropicalization. Its matroid-of-lines/adjoint constructions are directly useful for understanding hyperplanes inside Trop(M) and potential duality operations.

title: Tropical Incidence Relations, Polytopes, and Concordant Matroids
why_relevant: Develops tropical incidence relations characterizing containment of one tropical linear space in another. This is foundational for translating inclusion in Dr(M) into valuated-matroid or polyhedral conditions.

title: Tropical flag varieties
why_relevant: Proves equivalences among valuated flag matroids, flags of projective tropical linear spaces, and coherent flag matroidal subdivisions. Since chains in Dr(M) are flags of tropical subspaces, this supplies the main flag-Dressian framework.

title: Flag matroids with coefficients
why_relevant: Extends flag matroids to tract/hyperfield coefficients and treats duality and minors. It gives an algebraic framework in which flags of tropical linear spaces, quotients, and duality can be studied uniformly.

title: Polyhedra and parameter spaces for matroids over valuation rings
why_relevant: Gives polyhedral and moduli descriptions of valuated matroids and flags over valuation rings. Its tropical Bott-Samelson/flag viewpoint is relevant to quotient chains inside a fixed tropical linear space.

title: Quotients of M-convex sets and M-convex functions
why_relevant: Unifies quotients of matroids, valuated matroids, polymatroids, and M-convex functions. Since Dr(M) is naturally a quotient poset of valuated matroids, its equivalent quotient criteria are likely useful for constructing or obstructing an order-reversing involution.

title: Generalized permutahedra and positive flag Dressians
why_relevant: Studies valuated matroids, tropical incidence relations, flag matroids, and subdivisions into generalized permutahedra. It contains structural results on flag Dressians and incidence that specialize to codimension-one relative Dressian questions.

title: On local Dressians of matroids
why_relevant: Analyzes the Dressian of valuated matroids with fixed underlying matroid and its fan structure via matroid subdivisions. Although local Dressians differ from relative Dressians, their subdivision techniques are important for understanding the ambient valuated-matroid geometry.

title: Tropical Linear Spaces
why_relevant: Foundational source on tropical linear spaces, tropical Plucker vectors, dualization, stable intersection, and examples. The dualization operation here is a natural candidate or comparison point for any order-reversing operation on Dr(M).

title: The Tropical Grassmannian
why_relevant: Introduces the tropical Grassmannian and its relation to tropicalized linear spaces and Plucker relations. It is basic background for comparing realizable tropical subspaces with all valuated-matroid subspaces in a Dressian.

title: Dressians, Tropical Grassmannians, and Their Rays
why_relevant: Studies Dressians as parameter spaces of tropical linear spaces and develops their secondary-fan structure. Its ray and subdivision methods are useful for constructing explicit examples or counterexamples to poset-duality claims.

title: Matroid polytopes, nested sets and Bergman fans
why_relevant: Gives a detailed construction of Bergman fans from matroid polytopes and the lattice of flats. This is directly relevant to the embedding of flats into Dr(M) and to how contractions/localizations appear inside Trop(M).

title: The Bergman complex of a matroid and phylogenetic trees
why_relevant: Shows how the Bergman complex is controlled by the order complex of the lattice of flats. This is useful for understanding the flat-lattice part of Dr(M) that the desired involution must extend.

title: Local tropical linear spaces
why_relevant: Describes tropical linear spaces locally via matroid subdivisions and mixed subdivisions. These local models can help analyze how a proposed involution would behave on cells or stars of subspaces in Dr(M).

title: Representing tropical linear spaces by circuits
why_relevant: Gives circuit-based descriptions of tropical linear spaces. Circuit and cocircuit descriptions are often the most effective way to test containment, duality, and quotient conditions.

title: Algorithms for tight spans and tropical linear spaces
why_relevant: Provides computational methods for tropical linear spaces and duals of matroid subdivisions. Useful for testing candidate involutions on explicit examples of Dr(M).

title: Matroidal representations of groups
why_relevant: Develops tropical linear spaces over idempotent semifields and includes an orthogonal-complement operation via valuated matroid duality. This is relevant to any attempt to build a polarity-like involution on tropical subspaces.

title: A module-theoretic approach to matroids
why_relevant: Interprets matroids and strong maps using idempotent module theory and tropical linear spaces. Its categorical treatment of strong maps, stable sum, and stable intersection may help formulate an involution functorially.

title: Self-dual matroids from canonical curves
why_relevant: Studies self-dual matroids and self-dual valuated matroids, including the tropical Hodge star map. It is relevant for understanding when matroid self-duality or polarity lifts to valuated/tropical data.

title: Adjoints of Matroids
why_relevant: Modern treatment of matroid adjoints, opposite lattices, modular matroids, and adjoint sequences. Since an order-reversing involution on flats is a strong adjoint/polarity phenomenon, these results are central.

title: Adjoints of a Geometry
why_relevant: Introduces adjoints of geometric lattices and relates them to extensions. This is the classical source for the lattice-theoretic polarity problem that the question tropicalizes.

title: On Adjoints and Dual Matroids
why_relevant: Studies the relationship between ordinary matroid duality and lattice-theoretic adjoints, including negative examples. Useful for anticipating obstructions to extending a flat-lattice polarity to a larger quotient poset.

title: Polarity and Inner Products in Oriented Matroids
why_relevant: Develops polarity and inner-product analogues in oriented matroids and shows failures of classical polar-duality properties. Provides useful analogies and cautionary examples for tropical polarity.

title: Polarity and point extensions in oriented matroids
why_relevant: Relates polarities of oriented matroids to extension posets and incidence properties. This is close in spirit to extending a flat-lattice order reversal to a poset of subobjects.

title: On pseudomodular matroids and adjoints
why_relevant: Compares set-theoretic and lattice-theoretic duality and constructs higher-rank matroids with adjoints. Its Levi/intersection-property viewpoint is relevant to incidence obstructions in Dr(M).

title: Matroid adjoints and the minimum rank of zero-nonzero matrix patterns
why_relevant: Reformulates matroid adjoints via transpose patterns and dual lattices. This gives a modern combinatorial way to analyze adjoints/polarities that may interact well with quotient-poset constructions.

title: Uniform semimodular lattices and valuated matroids
why_relevant: Gives a lattice-theoretic characterization of integer-valued valuated matroids extending the classical flat-lattice characterization of matroids. This is relevant to lifting an order-reversing flat-lattice symmetry to valuated matroid data.

title: Matroids over partial hyperstructures
why_relevant: Provides a unified hyperfield/tract framework for ordinary, oriented, and valuated matroids, including duality and minors. This framework is useful for treating tropical linear spaces and polar duality algebraically.

title: Lorentzian polynomials
why_relevant: Establishes the Lorentzian/M-convex framework underlying many recent results on valuated matroids and tropical incidence. Its convexity and tropicalization techniques feed directly into modern work on relative Dressians.

title: Valuated matroids
why_relevant: The foundational paper defining valuated matroids and their projective equivalence. Since tropical linear spaces and Dr(M) are encoded by valuated matroids and quotients, this is essential background.
